growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.strain: Donghu Lake (East Lake, Wuhan) isolate; 16S rDNA identified (Zhou et al., 2010)
growth_records.medium: modified BG-11, P-limited (10 uM K2HPO4 = 4.3% P of standard BG-11)

up.method: calculated (Droop steady-state: qP = D x POP/POC)
not reported as biomass-specific rate; carbon substrate is dissolved CO2 at 400 uatm

growth_records.cite: Cheng, Kai et al. Cyanophage Propagation in the Freshwater Cyanobacterium Phormidium Is Constrained by Phosphorus Limitation and Enhanced by Elevated pCO2. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00617. [PMC6449453]
growth_records.snippet: we cultured Phormidium in P-limited chemostats under ambient (400 uatm) and elevated (800 uatm) pCO2 at growth rates of 0.6, 0.3, and 0.05 d-1. ... POC 970 (0.6 d-1, 400 uatm), POP 4.1 uM.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: specific growth rate = dilution rate; D=0.6 d-1 = 0.6/24 = 0.025 h-1; doubling time = ln2/0.6 = 1.155 d = 27.7 h. Phosphate uptake derived from Droop steady-state relation qP = mu x cell-quota = D x (POP/POC): 0.6/d x 4.1 uM / 970 uM C = 2.46/970 = 0.002536 mol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1 = 2.54 mmol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1. POC/POP measured (Table 1, n=3). Residual dissolved inorganic P <0.01 uM (P-limited). Reservoir P=10 uM K2HPO4. Batch max growth rate ~0.72 d-1. Full BG-11 salt composition not tabulated in text.

growth_records.strain: Donghu Lake (East Lake, Wuhan) isolate; 16S rDNA identified (Zhou et al., 2010)
growth_records.medium: modified BG-11, P-limited (10 uM K2HPO4 = 4.3% P of standard BG-11)

up.method: calculated (Droop steady-state: qP = D x POP/POC)

growth_records.cite: Cheng, Kai et al. Cyanophage Propagation in the Freshwater Cyanobacterium Phormidium Is Constrained by Phosphorus Limitation and Enhanced by Elevated pCO2. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00617. [PMC6449453]
growth_records.snippet: at growth rates of 0.6, 0.3, and 0.05 d-1 ... 800 uatm, 0.6 d-1: POC 1170 uM, POP 3.7 uM (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: mu=D=0.6 d-1 = 0.025 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.6 = 1.155 d = 27.7 h. qP = D x POP/POC = 0.6/d x 3.7/1170 = 2.22/1170 = 0.001897 mol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1 = 1.90 mmol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1. POC 1170, POP 3.7 uM measured (Table 1). Elevated pCO2 (800 uatm).

growth_records.snippet: growth rates of 0.6, 0.3, and 0.05 d-1 ... 800 uatm, 0.05 d-1: POC 2724 uM, POP 7.6 uM (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: mu=D=0.05 d-1 = 0.002083 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.05 = 332.7 h. qP = 0.05/d x 7.6/2724 = 0.38/2724 = 0.0001395 mol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1 = 0.140 mmol P (mol C)^-1 d^-1. POC 2724, POP 7.6 measured (Table 1). Highest C:P (371) here indicating strongest P-limitation under elevated pCO2.

growth_records.strain: Donghu Lake (East Lake, Wuhan) isolate; 16S rDNA identified (Zhou et al., 2010)
growth_records.cite: Cheng, Kai et al. Cyanophage Propagation in the Freshwater Cyanobacterium Phormidium Is Constrained by Phosphorus Limitation and Enhanced by Elevated pCO2. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00617. [PMC6449453]
growth_records.snippet: Batch mono-cultures were diluted 1:10 (v:v) weekly using sterile filtered standard BG-11 medium to keep the cells exponentially growing (maximum growth rate of about 0.72 d-1).
growth_records.notes: Reported maximum specific growth rate ~0.72 d-1 in nutrient-replete standard BG-11 batch. Converted: 0.72/24 = 0.030 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.72 = 0.963 d = 23.1 h. This is mu_max (used to scale chemostat dilution rates: 0.6/0.3/0.05 d-1 = 83/50/17% of mu_max ~0.72). No uptake/secretion rates for batch.
